analysis_result: 2/14 sporadic benign neurofibromas (14.3%) harbored somatic NF1 mutations. Matches the paper's reported 2/14.
denominator_used: N = 14; definition: sporadic benign neurofibromas (NF1 Status=='Sporadic' AND Tumor Type in ('Neurofibroma', 'Cutaneous_NF')) with WES data (non-null WES label in MOESM4). Barcodes: Tumor_1377, Tumor_1397, Tumor_1467, Tumor_1543, Tumor_1547, Tumor_1682, Tumor_1869, Tumor_1890, Tumor_2300, Tumor_2314, Tumor_2414, Tumor_2525, Tumor_781, Tumor_882
assumptions_made: WES available = non-null 'WES label' in MOESM4 (this yields 14; using WES=='X' strictly would yield 13 because NF1869 has a WES label but WES column is NaN); non-synonymous Variant_Classification set: Missense_Mutation, Nonsense_Mutation, Nonstop_Mutation, Frame_Shift_Del, Frame_Shift_Ins, In_Frame_Del, In_Frame_Ins, Splice_Site, Splice_Region, Translation_Start_Site; barcode = 'Tumor_' + WES label as an integer (no decimal); samples with WES labels not present in MAF are counted in denominator as WES samples with zero passing variants (not excluded)
step_by_step_analysis:
A. DATA LOADED
   - MOESM4 (Clinical Data): 108 rows x 15 cols; used columns Tumor_ID, Tumor Type, NF1 Status, WES, WES label
   - MOESM5 (MAF): 7457 rows x 123 cols; used columns Tumor_Sample_Barcode, Hugo_Symbol, Variant_Classification, FILTER

B. COHORT DEFINITION
   - Filter 1: NF1 Status == 'Sporadic' AND Tumor Type in ('Neurofibroma','Cutaneous_NF') -> 20 samples
   - Filter 2: WES label not null (WES data available) -> 14 samples (matches paper's n=14)
   - Note: WES=='X' strictly would return 13 (NF1869 has WES label 1869 but WES column NaN; its mutations ARE present in MOESM5 as Tumor_1869)
   - Final denominator: 14

C. VARIABLE CODING
   - Barcode derivation: 'Tumor_' + WES label (label converted to integer string)
   - Non-synonymous Variant_Classification values included: Missense_Mutation, Nonsense_Mutation, Nonstop_Mutation, Frame_Shift_Del, Frame_Shift_Ins, In_Frame_Del, In_Frame_Ins, Splice_Site, Splice_Region, Translation_Start_Site (Silent excluded)
   - FILTER=='PASS' (all 7457 rows in MOESM5 are PASS, so this filter is a no-op here)

D. COMPUTATION
   - Numerator: unique sporadic-benign-NF barcodes with >=1 NF1 non-synonymous PASS variant = 2
   - Denominator: sporadic benign NF with WES label = 14
   - NF1-mutated barcodes: ['Tumor_1869', 'Tumor_2525']
   - NF1 variants observed:
     * Tumor_1869: Missense_Mutation p.Y489C
     * Tumor_2525: Missense_Mutation p.I766S

E. RESULT
   - 2/14 sporadic benign neurofibromas (14.29%) harbored somatic NF1 mutations
   - Matches the paper's reported 2/14 (14.3%)

F. CAVEATS
   - Denominator uses presence of WES label (not strict WES=='X') to include NF1869 (its variants are in MOESM5); this exactly matches the paper's n=14
   - FILTER=='PASS' is trivial because 100% of MOESM5 rows are PASS (all non-passing calls were already removed)
   - Samples with WES but no NF1 variants (and/or no passing variants at all) are included in the denominator as zero-mutation samples
   - Splice_Region considered non-synonymous (conservative inclusion; no Splice_Region variants occur in this subset so does not affect result)
